item.title: Indigenous veteran Di Ryder slams 'distracting' booing at Anzac Day Welcome to Country
item.description: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas says algal bloom 'gone' but fishing restrictions need to expand
item.description: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australia due to the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks, but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up".

item.title: Four easy egg recipes for any occasion
item.description: If you're looking to add the humble egg into your weekly meals, we have you covered.

item.title: Two young girls die after Viveash crash, another three road fatalities over long weekend
item.description: Two young girls, aged three and four, die more than a week after a car crash in Viveash, with the news emerging after another three fatalities on WA roads over the Anzac Day long weekend.

item.title: Snowfall in April, record hot day in May. Is this a new norm for Tasmania? Scientists explain
item.description: Tasmania is well into autumn, but firefighters remain on alert as late-season bushfires have threatened communities. This Friday marks the first day of May, and with it could come one of the state's hottest May days in nearly 140 years of record-keeping.

item.title: RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies after elders booed on Anzac Day
item.description: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

item.title: AFL concedes error on controversial ARC call as league boss says umpiring 'as good as it's been for a long time'
item.description: AFL football boss Greg Swann says most of the feedback he receives from the clubs surrounding the officiating in the league is positive despite a pair of controversial review calls in Round 7.

item.title: Lebanon and Hezbollah at odds over peace talks with Israel as strikes continue despite ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's president has accused Hezbollah of hypocrisy, as the Iranian-backed militant group demanded the government walk away from peace talks with Israel.

item.title: Train collision near Jakarta kills 14 as rescuers complete evacuation
item.description: The train operator says the crash, on the outskirts of Jakarta, involved a commuter train and a long-distance train.

item.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far
item.description: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

item.title: The best ways to protect credit and debit cards from fraud
item.description: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Soaring petrol prices force tourism operators to introduce surcharges
item.description: Fuel costs have more than doubled for one of the Great Barrier Reef's major tourism operators — and it's forcing a rethink on how paradise is powered.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Learn why tomorrow's inflation data could be important for you in just three minutes
item.description: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

item.title: Scenic Rim community divided over proposed Cleanaway waste-to-energy incinerator
item.description: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert divides the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
item.description: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

item.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
item.description: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

item.title: White House blames ‘left-wing’ critics for attempt on Trump’s life
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
item.description: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

item.title: ASX loses ground, Bank of Japan keeps rates at 0.75pc — as it happened
item.description: Australia's share market lost ground on Tuesday, and Brent crude oil futures rose back above $US100 a barrel, while the Bank of Japan kept its overnight rate at 0.75pc. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Indigenous players defend St Kilda coach Ross Lyon after report reveals comment during training session
item.description: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

item.title: Iran blames US for failed peace talks during visit to Russia — as it happened
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says the US's "excessive demands" were the cause of failed peace talks. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has discussed the latest Iranian proposal. Look back on our live blog.

item.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
item.description: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Former Australian federal commissioner Tom Rogers to lead independent review into SA election
item.description: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
item.description: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Yarra River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
item.description: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
item.description: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

item.title: Why NSW hasn’t met a problem Tiana Penitani Gray couldn’t solve
item.description: Tiana Penitani Gray has never played second row before, but as the Origin stalwart prepares to play her third position in as many series such a bold change is nothing new.

item.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
item.description: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

item.title: Alex Johnston and Jarome Luai set to join PNG Chiefs in major get for NRL expansion team
item.description: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

item.title: Locust swarms in the Murtho area
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Locusts seen on Riverland farm
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bus driver's trial over fatal Gumlu crash continues in Townsville
item.description: The first police officers to arrive on the scene of a fatal bus crash in north Queensland have told the court of their conversations with the driver in the immediate aftermath of the incident.

item.title: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa spared jail over child abuse material
item.description: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa is spared jail after a court found her novel fetishised young children.

item.title: Perth man David Moussa pleads not guilty to stabbing father to death on grounds of insanity
item.description: David Moussa, 32, pleads not guilty on grounds of insanity to murdering his father in the Perth suburb of Clarkson.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
item.description: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
item.description: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

item.title: Saudi Arabia's Reality Check
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man pleads guilty in Austria to plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
item.description: A 21-year-old man has pleaded guilty over a jihadist plot to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Austria, which led to three of the star's shows being cancelled in 2024.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
item.description: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

item.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
item.description: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: Hundreds of child abuse files found on devices belonging to police killer Dezi Freeman
item.description: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

item.title: Plastic packaging price pain could push up costs for shoppers on everything from milk to make-up
item.description: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

item.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
item.description: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

item.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
item.description: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

item.title: What we know about the night 5yo girl Sharon Granites went missing in Alice Springs
item.description: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
item.description: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

item.title: Paid advertisements used to promote fraudulent investment platforms on social media, warns ASIC
item.description: The Sydney dad believed he was investing in cryptocurrency, stocks and even gold with the hopes of buying a home.

item.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
item.description: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

item.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
item.description: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their major off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

item.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery says her death was preventable
item.description: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

item.title: Video of arson attack on Central Coast Pilates studio released by police
item.description: The video released by police captures the moment a man douses the Pilates studio in petrol before setting it alight.

item.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe
item.description: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

item.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
item.description: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

item.title: Australian actor Megan Wilding stars in Game. Set. Match, a play that feels like tennis
item.description: What starts as a rom-com set at the Australian Open turns into a searing indictment of race relations in Australia in the new show Game. Set. Match.

item.title: Jarome Luai's move to Papua New Guinea makes the power of the Chiefs very real
item.description: Jarome Luai's decision to sign with Papua New Guinea is a showcase of the economic power behind the Chiefs and marks the start of the greatest rugby league gold rush since Super League.

item.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
item.description: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.

item.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted in probe over seashells photo
item.description: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her phone to avoid handing over key texts as part of defamation trial
item.description: Ms Wilson's lawyers have previously told Federal Court defamation hearing that her phone was stolen and she was unable to produce all of her WhatsApp message history.

item.title: Health Minister says Canberra Hospital knife incident will be subject to a joint review
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer says the knife produced by a Canberra Hospital patient that led to an hours-long security incident had not been detected by police during a search of the man earlier in the day.

item.title: Three people die in separate incidents in Toowoomba
item.description: A workplace death, a fatal truck collision on the Toowoomba bypass, and an elderly man killed in a fire have made for a tragic 24 hours in the regional city.

item.title: Keith Lees faces court over alleged murder of Meaghan Louise Rose on Sunshine Coast in 1997
item.description: Police allege Keith Lees killed Meaghan Louise Rose, whose body was found at the base of cliffs on the Sunshine Coast in July 1997.

item.title: New South Wales still committed to saving Tomago Aluminium as negotiations stall
item.description: The NSW government remains committed to Tomago Aluminium but is still waiting to learn how much the bailout will cost.

item.title: Tigers, Eagles or Cockle Divers — what makes up an Australian Rules football club's name?
item.description: How have Australia's football clubs come to be named, and what do those names represent? We analysed over 1,500 senior Aussie Rules clubs to find the patterns in how clubs have named themselves.

item.title: Brodie Dewar, accused killer of Labor identity Tim Picton, pleads not guilty to manslaughter
item.description: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
item.description: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

item.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
item.description: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

item.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
item.description: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

item.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
item.description: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

item.title: Illicit cigarettes, vapes, cash seized in raids on Canberra's black market tobacco trade
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer wants a sustained crackdown on the black market tobacco trade, as authorities swoop on six Canberra businesses.

item.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
item.description: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

item.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
item.description: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

item.title: Goldfields defensive driving course helps teens cope with unique road challenges
item.description: A new defensive driving course being offered in Kalgoorlie aims to equip young motorists with the skills to cope with the unique challenges of outback driving.

item.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
item.description: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

item.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
item.description: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

item.title: Police explore link between alleged kidnappings and Melbourne hospitality attacks
item.description: Police say the trio are linked to targeted attacks on promoters of a weekly event held in South Melbourne.

item.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
item.description: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

item.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
item.description: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

item.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
item.description: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

item.title: Back Roads: Navigating bull dust and racing the sun on the Binns Track
item.description: Journeying through the Binns Track in remote central Australia, Rae Johnston learns why you should always travel with mates.

item.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
item.description: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

item.title: 'Free Palestine' party pitched by YouTuber Avi Yemini to funnel votes to conservatives
item.description: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

item.title: Lebanon ceasefire not working because free rein given to Israel, says deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri
item.description: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 1: Are you a vexillologist in the making?
item.description: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

item.title: The vine with a murky past that is strangling Vanuatu's forests
item.description: A habitat-destroying invasive species is taking over Vanuatu's forests, but one village is fighting back and hoping to earn an income for its people at the same time.

item.title: Will the OPEC oil cartel survive without the UAE?
item.description: The United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for the past 66 years.

item.title: CCTV shows moment Jewish man is stabbed on the street
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: New study suggests majority of regional Australians back renewable energy transition
item.description: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

item.title: Antisemitism and social cohesion royal commission launched after Bondi Beach terror attack to release interim report
item.description: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will deliver its first report, two months after it began and less than six months after the Bondi Beach terror attack.

item.title: Six WA Police officers sanctioned over treatment of Aboriginal man who died in Kalgoorlie prison
item.description: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

item.title: Australians urged to stand up for Murray River as 'transformational moment' looms
item.description: Julie Jones has lived by the River Murray all her life and wants all South Australians to participate in the first comprehensive review of the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

item.title: Travis Head helps Sunrisers score six-wicket IPL win over Indians
item.description: Travis Head reminds cricket fans of what he can produce with the bat, as he helps Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket win over Mumbai Indians.

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Blues out to rewrite Women's State of Origin history against Maroons in Newcastle series opener
item.description: The Blues are yet to enjoy success in Newcastle, a fact they are mindful of ahead of the first Women's State of Origin match against the Maroons.

item.title: Bodycam footage shows police officers subduing London attacker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion interim report delivers 14 recommendations
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion releases its interim report, making 14 recommendations.

item.title: Perth wakes shrouded in thick fog as Main Roads issue traffic warning
item.description: Perth has been blanketed with thick fog, prompting warnings for motorists to use caution in the conditions.

item.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in primary school bathroom
item.description: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

item.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
item.description: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

item.title: ASIC drops case against Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter as search for buyer continues
item.description: It was alleged Liberty Bell Bay's former owner, British billionaire Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance, had failed to lodge financial returns for numerous years. The company then went into administration, and ASIC has dropped its legal case.

item.title: Ross Lyon clarifies Saints resignation offer over comment to First Nations players
item.description: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon is proud of the way the club reacted after he offended a group of the club's First Nations players.

item.title: What is inflation and should you worry about it?
item.description: The latest inflation data is making headlines. Find out what it means for you in three minutes.

item.title: Christchurch mosque attacker's conviction appeal application declined by New Zealand court
item.description: A New Zealand court declines an application by the Australian man who murdered 51 people at two mosques in 2019 to appeal his convictions.

item.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
item.description: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

item.title: US charges Sinaloa governor over alleged ties to Sinaloa Cartel and aiding drug trafficking
item.description: The indictment is a significant escalation of US anti-cartel action that is likely to increase tensions between the United States and Mexico.

item.title: Melbourne City to face 'team we never want to lose to' in A-League Women semifinal
item.description: Taylor Otto sees a lot of potential in the Australian domestic league. She thinks it just needs a bigger investment to get there.

item.title: Crows' skipper Jordan Dawson ruled out for Showdown after attending brother's funeral
item.description: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

item.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
item.description: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

item.title: NT Police find body believed to be missing 5yo Alice Springs girl
item.description: Northern Territory Police have found a body believed to be that of a missing five-year-old Alice Springs girl, five days after she disappeared from a town camp.

item.title: Paramedic stabbed in Reservoir in Melbourne's north
item.description: Police have charged a man with attempted murder after he allegedly stabbed an on-duty paramedic in Melbourne's north.

item.title: Victorian parliamentary inquiry into summer fires hears emotional pleas from Harcourt residents
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer hears the emotional plea of those impacted.

item.title: Rebel Wilson says social media posts are true during defamation trial cross-examination
item.description: Rebel Wilson tells a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor on her film The Deb are all true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
item.description: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

item.title: NSW Police under scrutiny over resources allocated to targeted Bondi Beach Hanukkah event
item.description: NSW Police officers assigned to patrol the Hanukkah celebration targeted in the Bondi Beach terrorist attack were told they did not need to "stay the entire duration" of the event, despite members of the Jewish community fearing it was at risk.

item.title: RACQ denies misleading customers with insurance notices amid ASIC Federal Court lawsuit
item.description: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla says it faced IDF interception
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Water and sewerage bill increases on the way for Tasmanians
item.description: Water bills for Tasmanian households and small businesses will rise by an average of 5.7 per cent each year for the next four years.

item.title: Savers Australia denies allegations of bed bugs at Brunswick thrift store
item.description: Thrift store chain Savers Australia has denied there is evidence of bed bugs at their popular Brunswick shop, as allegations of an outbreak continue to circulate online.

item.title: NSW strikes late to win Game I as Origin series heads to Queensland
item.description: NSW has come from behind to win the opening match of the Women's State of Origin series, and their coach is looking forward to trying to win the shield in Queensland.

item.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
item.description: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: NRL coach Craig Bellamy diagnosed with form of neurodegenerative disorder
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy has been diagnosed with a form of neurodegenerative disorder, the club has said.

item.title: WA Police seize guns from home with Nazi flags prominently displayed
item.description: Police raid a home as part of an operation to reassess licensed gun holders in the wake of the Bondi Beach shooting.

item.title: How two 'Origin plays' turned the tide for New South Wales against Queensland
item.description: With Queensland on the verge of another classic ambush, it took two "Origin plays" for New South Wales to turn the tide in the women's series opener in Newcastle.

item.title: Police use tear gas amid violent scenes outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police use tear gas amid violent scenes outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Guy Montgomery went from lazy school student to hosting a TV spelling bee across two countries
item.description: Guy Montgomery is back with the third season of his inimitable Spelling Bee, with brand-new sidekick Sam Campbell. We turn the tables on the Kiwi comic to ask him the tough questions.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a stunt gone wrong, a burger blue and a rescued roo
item.description: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

item.title: In a normal political world, the Liberal Party would be expecting a Nepean by-election win
item.description: With no Labor candidate contesting this weekend's Nepean by-election, it should be a cakewalk for the Victorian Liberals. But we are no longer in a normal political world.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: Philip O'Carroll speaks in an FCS home video.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Clancy walking in Melbourne
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tram passing school
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Philip O'Carroll teaching at FCS.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scott Kinnear and Jenny Lawlor kept documents from the 1990s.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'No secrets': Alternative school's history of discipline in spotlight
item.description: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

item.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says
item.description: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

item.title: Former Canberra registrar to face disciplinary action for perjury, attempting to pervert the course of justice
item.description: Former Canberra vascular registrar Imran Kader will face disciplinary action after he lied under oath and tried to interfere with a witness during criminal proceedings for an alleged rape.

item.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
item.description: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

item.title: Taylor says government is in 'denial' about situation in NT town camps — as it happened
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says the federal government is in "denial" about the situation in town camps following the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Saudi Arabia confirms it will no longer fund LIV Golf as future of league hangs in the balance
item.description: Australia's Cameron Smith is one of several big names in golf left with an uncertain future after Saudi Arabia pulled funding of LIV Golf.

item.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened
item.description: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

item.title: Extreme heat the most deadly yet preventable natural disaster, NSW Net Zero Commission says
item.description: An agency set up by the NSW government just two years ago says extreme heat is the most deadly yet most preventable natural hazard facing Australians.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Emma Thompson stars in a new film made for an orchestral performance of Holst’s The Planets
item.description: A new silent film starring Emma Thompson and Greg Wise will accompany the Sydney Symphony Orchestra and Holst’s The Planets in a world-premiere performance.

item.title: Those who joined the mining ventures also came to learn English.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Turning Strelley Station into a community was hard work.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Down a nondescript track off the highway, you might find Strelley.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bruce Thomas sings at the Pilbara strike commemoration.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: After leaving the stations, Aboriginal people formed independent mining cooperatives.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under influence of alcohol and drugs
item.description: Britney Spears has been charged in California with driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol.

item.title: Adelaide showed how good LIV Golf could be, but it was built on a flawed premise
item.description: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

item.title: Townsville man dies in fiery crash as road fatalities jump 24pc
item.description: Members of the public and emergency services assisted at the scene of a fatal crash in Townsville, one of several deadly crashes on Queensland roads over the past week.

item.title: Future of Northern Territory ambulance service in doubt as paramedics reach breaking point
item.description: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

item.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan
item.description: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

item.title: Mel C on her new album Sweat and the Spice Girls
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Victoria pledges to close legal 'loophole' to prevent attacks on paramedics
item.description: Ambulance Victoria and the paramedics union say some people who attack paramedics have escaped mandatory jail terms due to the law's definition of "on-duty".

item.title: Police say 14-year-old driver veered into oncoming traffic on Queensland's Fraser Coast
item.description: A 14-year-old driver and a toddler are among 10 people injured in a Fraser Coast crash.

item.title: A deadline for Trump to end the Iran war is looming. He may seek to get around it
item.description: A deadline for US President Donald Trump to end the Iran war is about to expire. Here is what to expect.

item.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says
item.description: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

item.title: Ricky Stuart says it is time Craig Bellamy put himself first after the NRL coach's shock diagnosis
item.description: Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says Craig Bellamy needs to start putting himself first after the Storm revealed the coaching legend had been diagnosed with a neurological condition.

item.title: AFL score reviews under scrutiny with some coaches questioning the point
item.description: Nobody wants to see their team robbed by a missed officiating decision. But as scrutiny over video assistant refereeing or umpiring continues, some are wondering whether it's all worth it.

item.title: Thick smoke rises from oil facility in Russia after Ukraine strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: More IP, more content, more jobs: What we learned at Screen Forever 40
item.description: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

item.title: Man found guilty of negligent driving causing death over crash with motorcyclist on Canberra road
item.description: Sean Joshua Walton was driving his truck and turning right at an intersection when he crashed into a black Harley Davidson motorbike being driven by 47-year-old Craig O'Neil.

item.title: Terrorism threat level rises, man arrested after stabbing of two Jewish men in the UK
item.description: Britain raises its national terrorism threat level to "severe" from "substantial", a day after an antisemitic stabbing attack in north London, as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vows stronger action to protect Jewish people.

item.title: Dyson Daniels and Mitchell Robinson ejected in Hawks' 140-89 loss to Knicks in NBA play-offs
item.description: There is little support for the Hawks' Australian star Dyson Daniels after he is ejected from Game 6 of their NBA play-off game against the Knicks.

item.title: Trump not keen on wearing body armour as Secret Service says agent not shot by friendly fire
item.description: US President Donald Trump has declined to wear a bulletproof vest amid fresh concerns about his safety following a shooting at a gala dinner on Saturday.

item.title: Banksy confirmed behind new London statue of man blinded by flag
item.description: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

item.title: Outback WA shire demands trucks get off road after beloved teacher killed in crash
item.description: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

item.title: Family of allegedly murdered 5yo calls for calm as police reveal vigilante attack left suspect unconscious
item.description: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

item.title: Suns coach Damien Hardwick fires back at Saints' Ross Lyon's AFL draft comment
item.description: Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick provides a spirited response to St Kilda counterpart Ross Lyon's comment about the AFL national draft.

item.title: Supreme Court finds Perth man David Moussa not guilty of father's murder on insanity grounds
item.description: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

item.title: Port Pirie locals worried about jobs as Nyrstar smelter's future in doubt
item.description: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

item.title: Kingswood Primary School teacher accused of installing bathroom camera denied bail
item.description: Parents of children attending Kingswood Primary School filled the Moorabbin Magistrates Court on Friday, intending to see accused teacher Timothy Blamires.

item.title: Beaches closed near Wollongong as whale carcass removed at Bellambi
item.description: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

item.title: Kalgoorlie couple who underwent IVF treatment in shock after welcoming quadruplets
item.description: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

item.title: Former ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker talks to Stateline
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Melbourne Storm lose seven in a row for the first time in club history
item.description: The Melbourne Storm have sunk to a historic low, losing seven consecutive NRL matches for the first time as the Dolphins powered to victory at a wet Lang Park in Brisbane.

item.title: Rebel Wilson defamation trial told Charlotte MacInnes was 'fudging' her version of producer incident
item.description: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

item.title: Family stunned after welcoming quadruplets in rare IVF birth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for killer Andrew Mungatopi
item.description: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

item.title: Planning and Environment Court approves Sheraton high-rise development for Hervey Bay
item.description: Activists appealed the local council's approval of the 18-storey resort on the grounds that it would endanger local wildlife. The community fears the court's green light for the development will permanently change their seaside town.

item.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as unseasonably warm weather continues
item.description: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

item.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute as Freo downs Dogs
item.description: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says
item.description: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.
